name: Booksy Appointment Booking API
what_it_does: Beauty and barbershop appointment booking marketplace with API — enabling salons, barbershops, spas, and beauty professionals to manage online bookings, client management, payments, and marketing, with a consumer marketplace for clients to discover and book beauty services globally, primarily serving the professional beauty industry with emphasis on hair salons and barbershops.
use_cases: Integrating Booksy booking data via API for CRM and business analytics
Managing business appointments via Booksy API for multi-location salon management
Syncing Booksy calendar data via API for staff scheduling and resource planning
Accessing client history via Booksy API for personalized marketing automation
Building white-label booking widgets via Booksy API for salon websites
not_for: Medical/clinical appointment scheduling (use SimplePractice for healthcare)
Enterprise resource booking beyond beauty services
Restaurant or hospitality reservations (Booksy is beauty/wellness-specific)
best_when: A hair salon, barbershop, or beauty professional needs a mobile-first booking platform with marketplace visibility and consumer-facing app for client acquisition.
avoid_when: You need healthcare scheduling, enterprise resource management, or services outside the beauty/wellness industry.

auth.notes: OAuth2 for partner integrations. API access typically requires Booksy partner agreement. Limited public API documentation — primarily for approved partners and integrators. Consumer-facing API for the Booksy app. Marketplace model: business data accessible via partner API. API access not self-serve — requires contacting Booksy partnerships team.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free basic plan; transaction fees apply for payments
pricing.paid_tiers: Boost plans for marketing visibility; pricing varies by region
pricing.notes: Polish company (Warsaw). Founded 2013 (Stefan Batory). $70M+ funding (Sequoia, Bessemer). 40,000+ businesses on platform. 10M+ consumers. Global expansion: US, UK, Spain, Germany, Brazil, South Africa. Barbershop focus: heavy adoption in US barbershop market. Booksy Biz: business app. Consumer: Booksy app for discovery. POS integration. Team scheduling. Inventory management. Marketing tools. COVID-driven growth (contactless booking). US market: major barbershop chain adoption.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API access requires partner agreement — not self-serve. Limited public documentation. Polish company (GDPR). OAuth2 for partners. Consumer marketplace model. $70M+ funded. Barbershop focus.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Appointment reads idempotent by appointment ID. Limited API documentation makes full idempotency behavior uncertain.
known_agent_gotchas: API requires partner agreement — contact partnerships@booksy.com for access
Not self-serve API: no documentation for immediate integration without partnership
Consumer marketplace model: client-facing discovery via Booksy app (not business-to-business API)
Polish company — GDPR applies; EU/US data residency available
Primary use case is business-facing app (Booksy Biz), not API integration
Barbershop/beauty focus: not suitable for general appointment scheduling use cases

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. OAuth2 for partner integrations. GDPR compliant (Polish company). Client PII (name, contact, services booked) in booking data. US/EU data residency. Partner agreement required — reduces anonymous API access risk. Consumer marketplace: client data shared between business and Booksy platform.
